name: Kanuri
notes:
Kanuri (Kanuri: Kanuri) — Saharan (Nilo-Saharan) language of Borno (Nigeria), Niger, Chad and Cameroon. INPUT CONTRACT: the Standard Kanuri Orthography (SKO), the Latin orthography based on the Maiduguri/Yerwa dialect, developed by the Kanuri Research Unit and Kanuri Language Board and approved in Maiduguri in 1975 (also the basis of the Niger arrêté 213-99 alphabet). Ajami (Arabic-script) Kanuri is OUT OF SCOPE. The SKO letter set is: a b c d e ǝ f g h i j k l m n ny o p r ɍ s sh t u w y z. `kr` is a macrolanguage code (Yerwa/Central and Manga Kanuri); this map follows Yerwa (Maiduguri), the standard variety. KEY FEATURES: (1) DIGRAPHS: = /t͡ʃ/, = /d͡ʒ/, = /ʃ/, = /ɲ/ (Hutchison lists c, j, sh, ny as the palatal series of the SKO). (2) PRENASALIZED STOPS are written as digraphs in the SKO; also spells plain [ŋ], an allophone of /n/ before velars (the velar stop is often deleted). (3) SCHWA: <ǝ> is the central mid vowel /ə/ (encoded U+01DD in Kanuri practice, not U+0259; both code points are accepted as input keys here); [ɨ] is an allophone of /ə/. (4)

is not a phoneme but is a letter of the SKO: it is the realization of /b/ before a voiceless plosive (náb+t -> nápt) and varies freely with /f/ in some ideophones. (5) LENITION allophones, largely predictable and partly reflected in spelling: /b/ -> [β] and /f/ -> [ɸ] intervocalically/in open syllables (the SKO often writes the weakened result as ), /ɡ/ -> [ɣ] intervocalically. (6) RETROFLEX: <ɍ> writes the retroflex liquid of Yerwa; its occurrence is otherwise predictable before high front /i/ (where /l/ -> [ɭ]), and in loanwords it is written . (7) TONE IS NOT WRITTEN in the SKO (Hutchison states this explicitly); Kanuri is tonal, so tone is NOT recoverable from standard orthography and is not emitted here. Glottal stop /ʔ/ is likewise not represented by a letter.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Kanuri language
sources.notes: Phoneme inventory, allophony (p, ɸ, β, ɣ, ɭ, ŋ), and the 1975 Standard Kanuri Orthography letter list.

sources.author: Hutchison, John P.
sources.title: Introduction to the Kanuri Language
sources.notes: Consonant/vowel schema presented in Standard Kanuri Orthography spellings: palatal series c, j, sh, ny; prenasalized mb, nd, ng; retroflex liquid; explicit statement that tone is not indicated in the SKO.

sources.author: Cyffer, Norbert & Hutchison, John P.
sources.title: Dictionary of the Kanuri Language
sources.notes: Reference lexicon written in the Standard Kanuri Orthography (ISBN 90-6765-412-4).

sources.author: Cyffer, Norbert
sources.title: A Sketch of Kanuri
sources.notes: Reference sketch grammar; source of the nasal/velar allophony statement.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Voiced retroflex lateral flap
sources.notes: Reports a retroflex lateral flap [ɭ̆] for Kanuri in Nigeria — the value used here for the letter <ɍ>.

ancestors.notes: Nilotic — the classification node this language sits under.

location.notes: Glottolog's computed centroid for the Kanuri-Kanembu family — a summary of a whole clade's range, not a place anyone speaks.
